title: Centring is not one thing
checkLabels.t11: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: One line in .page and two in .badge.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you work only in styles.css.
why.no-page: There is no element with the page class.
why.no-badge: There is no element with the badge class.
why.page-left: The content still sits against the left edge.
hint2.page-left: There is a ceiling on the width, so there is leftover space to divide.
why.page-width-lost: The 480 ceiling is gone. Without it there is no leftover and centring does nothing.
why.badge-text-left: The word in the badge sits against the left edge.
hint2.badge-text-left: This arranges content inside the box, it does not move the box.
why.badge-text-top: The word sits at the top rather than in the middle.
hint2.badge-text-top: For a single line there is an old approach: make the line height equal to the height of the box.
hint3.badge-text-top: The box is 64 with a 2 pixel border on each side, so 60 remain inside. The line height must be exactly that.
why.badge-resized: You changed the size of the badge. It stays 64 by 64.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red.
blocks.0.text: The content sits against the left edge, and the word in the round badge sits in its top left corner. Both look like the same problem. They are not.
blocks.2.items.0: Text inside a box, horizontally — you arrange the content inside.
blocks.2.items.1: A box inside its parent, horizontally — you move the box itself.
blocks.2.items.2: Text inside a box, vertically — there is a trick for one line and nothing for more.
blocks.2.items.3: A box inside its parent, vertically — the hardest, and for a long time there was no decent answer.
blocks.3.text: Each of the four needs a different property. That is exactly why centring has a reputation for being impossible: you learn one, try it on another, and it does nothing.
blocks.5.text: You already know this from lesson 22:
blocks.7.text: auto on the left and right means: split the leftover evenly. It only works with a width or a ceiling — otherwise the box fills the row and there is no leftover. Here the ceiling is 480, so on a wide screen there is empty space and it gets divided.
blocks.9.text: Horizontally that is text-align. Vertically, for a SINGLE line, there is an old approach: make the line height equal to the height of the box and the line lands in the middle of it.
blocks.11.text: It works perfectly for one line and falls apart at two: two lines of 60 make 120 and the text leaves the circle. So it is a trick, not a solution — used for buttons, badges and labels, where the content is one word and you are sure it will stay one.
blocks.12.text: Horizontal centring was solved in 1996. Vertical centring waited for flexbox, which is about twenty years.
blocks.14.text: Centre the page content horizontally and put the word in the badge exactly in its middle — both ways. The badge stays 64 by 64.
